source_quote: Mothers in the intervention group reported a significant reduction in the frequency of depressive symptoms (b = 20.98; 95% CI 21.53 to 20.41). The change was equivalent to 0.43 SD.
reasoning: The outcome is 'Depressive symptoms at 0-1 months' which is continuous data. The text reports a regression coefficient (b = 20.98) which appears to be -0.98 (likely OCR error with '20.98' meaning '-0.98'), with 95% CI -1.53 to -0.41. This represents the mean difference in depressive symptom frequency change between intervention and control groups. The negative value indicates improvement (reduction) in the intervention group.

source_quote: After six months, compared with the control group, the communication ability, housework handling ability, self-care ability and life quality of the patients in the intervention group were improved, and the quality of life and caring ability of their caregivers were also significantly improved.
reasoning: The outcome is 'Quality of life at 1-6 months' for Alzheimer Disease patients. source_quote: The treatment significantly affected both GHQ and NPI distress leading in both cases to a net reduction of slightly more that 1 point for GHQ and almost 2 points for NPI distress.

source_quote: Results showed a potential beneficial effect with a reduction of 2.13 points (95% CI (0.89 to 3.38), p<0.01, n=99) on the SRQ-20 and 1.98 points (95% CI (1.06 to 2.90), p<0.01, n=99) on the EPDS at the post-intervention time point for the intervention group compared with standard care.
reasoning: The outcome is 'Depressive symptoms at 0-1 months'. The EPDS (Edinburgh Postnatal Depression Scale) is the standard measure for depressive symptoms in perinatal populations. The text states 'a reduction of 1.98 points (95% CI 1.06 to 2.90)' in the intervention group compared to standard care. Since this is explicitly described as a reduction (lower scores are better for depression), the mean difference is -1.98 with the CI bounds also negative: [-2.90, -1.06].

source_quote: 1-month post-intervention, wellbeing scores (p-value 0.008) and self-care self-efficacy (0.049) scores were higher among intervention compared to control women.

source_quote: The secondary outcomes (ie, waist circumference, total cholesterol, low-density lipoprotein cholesterol, diabetes self-efficacy, diabetes self-management behaviors, diabetes knowledge and diabetes distress) were improved significantly in the intervention group as compared with the control group in the 12th month during the 12-month follow-up (−3.14 cm, P = 0.001; −0.30 mmol/L, P = 0.032; −0.25 mmol/L, P = 0.008; 0.87, P < 0.001; 10.67, P < 0.001; 3.42, P < 0.001; −4.97, P < 0.001).

source_quote: Over time, participants in both conditions showed a significant and dramatic decline in self-reported methamphetamine use (99% at baseline versus 53% at 12-months, p<0.0001) and significant increase in consistent condom use (32% baseline versus 44% at 12 months, p<0.0001).

source_quote: Forty-four patients were randomly assigned to either EVT with intensive exercise (intensive exercise group, n = 22) or EVT without intensive exercise (non-intensive exercise group, n = 22). Five patients in the intensive exercise group were lost to follow-up (four lacking clinical data and one who had withdrawn from the study), and one patient in the non-intensive exercise group died.
reasoning: The outcome is 'Mortality by end of follow-up'. The text explicitly states: 44 patients randomized (22 per group), 5 lost to follow-up in intensive exercise group (4 lacking data + 1 withdrawn, none described as deaths), and 1 patient in non-intensive exercise group died. Therefore: intervention group had 0 deaths out of 22, control group had 1 death out of 22. Using the randomized denominators as these represent the full sample at risk for mortality.
